SourceText: {F1_SON_5} and his mother are playing dress up during one-on-one time. Let’s see how they have fun together:
text: {F1_SON_5} kunye nomama wakhe badlala umdlalo wokunxibisana bebodwa. Makhe sibone ukuba bazonwabise njani kunye:

SourceText: **{F1_MOTHER}**: “I have 5 minutes of one-on-one time to spend with you, {F1_SON_5}. What would you like to do?”
text: **{F1_MOTHER}**. "Ndinemizuzu emi- 5 yokuchitha ixesha kunye nawe, {F1_SON_5} sisodwa. Yintoni ongathanda siyenze?"

SourceText: **{F1_SON_5}**: “I want to play dress up!”
text: **{F1_SON_5}**. "Ndifuna ukudlala umdlalo wokunxibisana!"

SourceText: **{F1_MOTHER}**: "Wonderful idea, {F1_SON_5}! I see you are taking out the yellow scarf with blue stripes on it.”
text: **{F1_MOTHER}**. "Umbono Omhle, {F1_SON_5}! Ndiyabona ukuba ukhupha isikhafu esityheli sinomgca oluhlaza okwesibhakabhaka kuso"

SourceText: **{F1_SON_5}**: "Mommy, it’s for you."
text: **{F1_SON_5}**: "Mama, sesakho."

SourceText: **{F1_MOTHER}**: "Thank you, I love it!"
text: **{F1_MOTHER}**: "Enkosi, Ndiyasithanda!"

SourceText: **{F1_SON_5}**: "Wear it, Mommy!"
text: **{F1_SON_5}**: "Sinxibe, Mama!"

SourceText: **{F1_MOTHER}**: “Look how good you are at getting dressed by yourself, {F1_SON_5}!”
text: **{F1_MOTHER}**. "Jonga ulunge kanjani ekuzinxibiseni ngokunokwakho, {F1_SON_5}!"

SourceText: **{F2_GRANDMOTHER}**: “I am so happy to see you playing quietly with your blocks, {F2_DAUGHTER_3}. When I come back, it will be time to get ready for bed. When you are ready for bed, we can have 5 minutes of one-on-one time together.”

SourceText: **{F2_DAUGHTER_3}**: “Okay!”
text: **{F2_DAUGHTER_3}**: "Kulungile!"

SourceText: **{F2_GRANDMOTHER}**: “Time to get ready for bed, {F2_DAUGHTER_3}. First, let’s get you nice and clean with a bath.”
text: **{F2_GRANDMOTHER}**:"Lixesha lokulungiselela ukuya ebhedini, {F2_DAUGHTER_3}. Okokuqala, mandikwenze ubemhle kwaye ucoceke ngokukuhlamba."

SourceText: **{F2_GRANDMOTHER}**: “You were so well-behaved in the bath, {F2_DAUGHTER_3}. Now it is time to put your pyjamas on.”
text: **{F2_GRANDMOTHER}**: "Uye waziphatha kakuhle ekukuhlambeni, {F2_DAUGHTER_3}. Ngoku lixesha lokuba unxibe impahla zokulala."

SourceText: **{F2_GRANDMOTHER}**: “Well done for putting on your pyjamas by yourself, {F2_DAUGHTER_3}! Now let’s brush your teeth. Make sure you get the ones in the back of your mouth too!”
text: **{F2_GRANDMOTHER}**:"Wenze kakuhle ngokuzinxibela impahla zokulala ngokunokwakho, {F2_DAUGHTER_3}! Ngoku masihlambe amazinyo akho. Qinisekisa ukuba uyafikelela nakula asemva komlomo wakho nawo!"

SourceText: **{F2_GRANDMOTHER}**: “You did so well getting ready for bed! Now, we have 5 minutes of one-on-one time to spend together. What would you like to do?”
text: **{F2_GRANDMOTHER}**: "Wenze kakuhle ngokulungiselela ukuya ebhedini! Ngoku, sinemizuzu emi- 5 yexesha sisodwa. Yintoni ongathanda siyenze?"

SourceText: **{F2_DAUGHTER_3}**: “Yay! Can you read me my favourite story?”
text: **{F2_DAUGHTER_3}**: "Yay! Ungandifundela ibali lam endilithandayo?"

SourceText: **{F2_GRANDMOTHER}**: “ “The end….” Such a lovely story! Goodnight, my baby girl. I love you very much.”
text: **{F2_GRANDMOTHER}**: "Isiphelo....." Ibali elimnandi kangaka! Busuku benzolo, sana lwam eliyintombi. Ndiyakuthanda kakhulu."

SourceText: **{F2_DAUGHTER_3}**: “Goodnight, {F2_GRANDMOTHER}.”
text: **{F2_DAUGHTER_3}**: "Busuku benzolo, {F2_GRANDMOTHER}.”

SourceText: 💡 Learn about different emotions. There are 6 basic emotions that each send us a very specific message:

SourceText: 😔Sad: Something important in your life needs attention.

SourceText: 🔴Angry: Stand up for ourselves and set limits.
text: 🔴Umsindo: Zimele ngokunokwakho kwaye umisele imida.

SourceText: 😣Disgusted: Move away from what we don't like.

SourceText: 👀Surprised: Stop and pay attention for a moment.

SourceText: 🚩Afraid: Run away from danger.
text: 🚩Ukoyika: Balekela kude kwingozi.

SourceText: This lesson is about how we can stay calm and manage our anger when dealing with stressful moments with our children. 💚
text: Esi isifundo simalunga nokuba singazigcina njani sizolile kunye nokulawula imisindo yethu xana sijongene nemizuzu yonxinzelelo kunye nabantwana bethu. 💚

SourceText: 🌷 We love our children, but stress from caregiving and other events can make us feel angry. This is a normal part of life!
text: 🌷 Siyabathanda abantwana bethu, kodwa uxinzelelo lokunakekela kunye nakwezinye iziganeko singasenza sizive sinomsindo. Lento yinto eqhelekileyo ebomini!

SourceText: 💡 It is important for us to try to stay calm in order to help manage our anger so we do not hurt others.
text: 💡 Kubalulekile kuthi ukuba sihlale sizibambile kwaye silawula imisindo ukwenzela singalimazi abanye.

SourceText: When I am angry at my child, I should show that I am angry so they stop misbehaving.
text: Xana ndinomsindo emntwaneni wam ofikisayo, Ndiyabonisa ukuba ndinomsindo ukwenzela bayeke ukuziphatha kakubi.

SourceText: 💛 It can be very difficult when your child is misbehaving, but you should try to manage your own stress and control your anger before doing anything.
text: 💛Kunganzima xana umntwana wakho ofikisayo eziphatha kakubi, kodwa kufuneka uzame ukulawula uxinzelelo kwaye sibambe umsindo wethu phambi kokwenza nantoni.

SourceText: 🌱 Taking deep breaths will help. Being angry may cause you to say or do things you will later regret.
text: 🌱Ukuphefumla kambalwa kunganceda. Ukubanomsindo kungenza utsho okanye wenze izinto ozakuthi uzisole.

SourceText: Let’s see how {F1_FATHER} and {F1_DAUGHTER_13} spend one-on-one time together:
text: Masibone ukuba u {F1_FATHER} kunye no{F1_DAUGHTER_13} bachitha njani ixesha bebobabini:

SourceText: **{F1_FATHER}**: “{F1_DAUGHTER_13}, how was your day?”
text: **{F1_FATHER}**: "{F1_DAUGHTER_13}, belunjani usuku lwakho?"

SourceText: **{F1_DAUGHTER_13}**: “My day was good, {F1_FATHER}. I just came from school.”
text: **{F1_DAUGHTER_13}**: "Usuku lwam belulungile, {F1_FATHER}. ndisandokubuya esikolweni."

SourceText: **{F1_FATHER}**: “I have 5 minutes of one-on-one time to spare before I have to drive to work again. What would you like to do together?”
text: **{F1_FATHER}**:"Ndinemizuzu emi- 5 yexesha lethu sobabini endinalo phambi kokuba ndiqhube ndiye emsebenzini kwakhona. Yintoni obungathanda siyenze kunye?"

SourceText: **{F1_DAUGHTER_13}**: “I was about to do my Science homework. The topic for the homework is to make something out of recycled materials. Could you please help me with it?”
text: **{F1_DAUGHTER_13}**:"Bendizakwenza umsebenzi we Nzululwazi. Isihloko salomsebenzi wasekhaya kukwenza into yoku risayikilisha imathiriyeli. Ungakwazi ukunceda ngayo?"

SourceText: **{F1_FATHER}**: “I would be happy to!”
text: **{F1_FATHER}**: "Ndingathanda ukwenza!"

SourceText: **{F1_DAUGHTER_13}**: “I thought of building a recycled house using old bottles, boxes, and other recycled materials. Look! I picked these up on my way home. They were thrown away by the side of the road and outside the spaza shops.”
text: **{F1_DAUGHTER_13}**: "Bendicinga ngokwakha indlu yerisayikila ndisebenzise iibhotile ezindala, ibhokisi, kunye nenye imathiriyeli. Jonga! Ndichole ezizinto endleleni ebuyela ekhaya. Bezilahlwe ecaleni kwendlela nangaphandle kwasevenkileni."

SourceText: **{F1_FATHER}**: “What a good idea, {F1_DAUGHTER_13}! Sounds like you’ve thought about this a lot and want to put a lot of effort into your homework. How do you want me to help you?”
text: **{F1_FATHER}**:"Ngumbono olungileyo lowo, {F1_DAUGHTER_13}! Kuvakala ingathi ucingisisile malunga nalento kakhulu kwaye ufuna ukufaka umzamo omkhulu  kulomsebenzi wasekhaya. Ufuna ndincede phi mna ukunceda wena?"

SourceText: **{F1_DAUGHTER_13}**: “Could you help me clean these bottles? They will be the pillars of the house. I will make the boxes into the walls and roof of the house. Later, I thought we could paint the boxes and bottles so they look beautiful.”
text: **{F1_DAUGHTER_13}**. "Ungakwazi ukundinceda ndicoce ezibhotile? Zizakuba ziintsika zalendlu. Ndizozenza iibhokisi zibe ziindonga kunye nophahla lwendlu. Kamva, bendicinga singakwazi ukupeyinta iibhokisi kunye neebhotile ukuze zibukeke kakuhle."

SourceText: **{F1_FATHER}**: “I may have some leftover paint outside the house. I can take a look later.”
text: **{F1_FATHER}**: " Ndinganayo enye ipeyinti eshekele umphandle wendlu. Ndingajonga kamva."

SourceText: (Later in the day)
text: (Kamva ngala suku)

SourceText: **{F1_FATHER}**: “Wow, it looks different now! It does not look like you used rubbish at all anymore. You also painted the walls and the roof beautifully! I think your teacher will be proud of you when she sees your homework!”
text: **{F1_FATHER}**."Wowu, ikhangeleka yohlukile ngoku! Ayibukeki ngathi usebenzise izinto esezisetyenzisiwe konke konke kwakhona. Uye wapeyinta nodonga kunye nophahla kakuhle! Ndicinga utitshala wakho uzozingca ngawe xana ebona umsebenzi wakho wasekhaya!"

SourceText: **{F1_DAUGHTER_13}**: “Thank you for your help, {F1_FATHER}. You know, there were a lot of fun things that happened in school today.”
text: **{F1_DAUGHTER_13}**: "Enkosi ngoncedo lwakho, {F1_FATHER}. Uyayazi, bekukho izinto ezininzi esikolweni ebezisonwabisa ezenzekileyo namhlanje."

SourceText: **{F1_FATHER}**: “Go ahead, tell me!”
text: **{F1_FATHER}**. "Qhubeka, xelela mna!"

text: **{F2_SON_15}**: “Aw…enkosi, {F2_GRANDFATHER}!”

text: **{F2_GRANDFATHER}**: "Kungakuhle ukuba singakwazi ukubane xesha kunye rhoqo. Singakhe sizame ukuyenza yonke imihla?"

text: **{F2_SON_15}**: "Ndicinga njalo.... nangona ndixakekile ngamanye amaxesha."

SourceText: **{F2_GRANDFATHER}**: “Yeah, that’s true. Let’s make it something that we can really do. I am sure we could find 15 minutes together!”

SourceText: **{F2_SON_15}**: “Sure, we can try.”
text: **{F2_SON_15}**: "Ngokuqinisekileyo, singazama."

SourceText: **{F2_GRANDFATHER}**: “When would be the best time for you?”
text: **{F2_GRANDFATHER}**:" Leliphi ixesha elilungileyo kuwe?"

SourceText: **{F2_SON_15}**: “How about after dinner?”
text: **{F2_SON_15}**: Kunganjani emva kwesidlo sangokuhlwa?"

SourceText: **{F2_GRANDFATHER}**: “That could work as long as we clean up first. Let’s start today. And remember, you can choose what we do!”
text: **{F2_GRANDFATHER}**: "Lonto ingasebenza nangoko sizakucoca kuqala. Masiqale namhalanje. Kwaye khumbula, ungakhetha into esiyenzayo!"

SourceText: **{F2_SON_15}**: “Okay, {F2_GRANDFATHER}. I look forward to it.”
text: **{F2_SON_15}**:"Kulungile, {F2_GRANDFATHER}. Ndijonge phambili kulonto."

SourceText: **{F2_GRANDFATHER}**: “Thanks, {F2_SON_15}. I really appreciate having this time with you. It means a lot to me.”
text: **{F2_GRANDFATHER}**: “Enkosi, {F2_SON_15}. Ndiyabulela ngenene ukuba sibenelixesha kunye. Ithetha lukhulu kum."

SourceText: {F1_FATHER} and {F1_DAUGHTER_16} are spending one-on-one time by taking a walk together. {F1_DAUGHTER_16} looks very upset.
text: U{F1_FATHER} kunye no{F1_DAUGHTER_16} bachitha ixesha bebobabini ngokuhamba kunye. U{F1_DAUGHTER_16} ubonakala ekhathazeke kakhulu.

SourceText: **{F1_FATHER}**: “{F1_DAUGHTER_16}, I noticed you have less energy today and you look sad. Do you want to talk about it?”
text: **{F1_FATHER}**: "{F1_DAUGHTER_16}, ndiqaphele ukuba unamandla amancinci namhlanje kwaye ukhangeleka lusizi. Uyafuna ukuthetha ngayo?"

SourceText: **{F1_DAUGHTER_16}**: “Oh, {F1_FATHER}. I studied so hard for my maths test and failed. I feel so sad and frustrated.”
text: **{F1_DAUGHTER_16}**: "Oh, {F1_FATHER}. Ndifunde nzima kuvavanyo lwam lwematematika ndaza ndatshona. Ndiziva ndilusizi kwaye ndikhathazekile."

SourceText: **{F1_FATHER}**: “That is frustrating, my girl. I know how hard you studied. Maybe we can come up with a new plan to help you with maths. Should we ask your teacher for extra help? Would you like me to talk to her or do you want to do it?”
text: **{F1_FATHER}**: "Iyacaphukisa lonto ke, ntombam. Ndiyayazi indlela ofunde ngayo. Mhlawumbi singaza nesicwangciso esitsha sokukunceda kwimathematika. Ingaba kufuneka sicele uncedo olongezelelweyo kutitshala wakho? Ngaba ungathanda ukuba ndithethe naye okanye ufuna ukuyenza ngokwakho?"

SourceText: **{F1_DAUGHTER_16}**: “Thank you, {F1_FATHER}. I can do it! I think that might be a good idea.”
text: **{F1_DAUGHTER_16}**: "Enkosi, {F1_FATHER}. Ndingakwazi ukuyenza! Ndicinga ukuba ingangumbono omhle lowo."

SourceText: **{F1_FATHER}**: “You know, maths may be difficult for you, but I am so proud of you for studying so hard, {F1_DAUGHTER_16}. Other people might give up, but you keep trying. That will help you so much in life!”
text: **{F1_FATHER}**: "Uyayazi, imathematika inokuba nzima kuwe, kodwa ndiyazingca ngawe ngokufunda nzima, {F1_DAUGHTER_16}. Abanye abantu bangancama, kodwa wena uqhubeke uzama. Oko kuya kukunceda kakhulu ebomini!"

SourceText: 💛 Accept how you and your teen feel. Remember, your body and their body are sending messages about what you both are feeling, and those messages are important.

SourceText: ⭐ Do some active exercise to shake out anger, give each other some space, or take some deep breaths to calm down. These are some ways to deal with your emotions.
text: ⭐ Yenza umthambo wokukhupha umsindo, ukunikana isithuba, okanye niphefumlele phezulu ukuze uzole. Kukho iindlela ezithile zokujongana neemvakalelo zakho.

SourceText: 🌷 Use actions and words to make your teen feel accepted and loved.
text: 🌷 Sebenzisa iintshukumo kunye namazwi ukwenza umntwana wakho ofikisayo azive emkelekile kwaye ethandwa.

SourceText: 💚 Remind your teen that you are there for them and that they can always talk to you.
text: 💚 Khumbuza umntwana ofikisayo wakho ukuba ukhona kubo kwaye bangathetha kunye nawe maxesha onke.

SourceText: This lesson is about how we can keep calm and manage our anger when dealing with stressful moments with our teens. 💚
text: Esi isifundo asimalunga nokuba uzigcina njani uzolile kunye nokulawula imisindo yethu xana umelana nemizuzu yonxinzelelo kunye nabantwana bethu abafikisayo. 💚

SourceText: 🌷 We love our teens, but stress from caregiving and other events can make us feel angry. This is a normal part of life!
text: 🌷 Siyabathanda abantwana bethu, kodwa uxinzelelo lokunakekela kunye nakwezinye iziganeko zingasenza sizive sinomsindo. Lento yinto eqhelekileyo ebomini!

SourceText: 💡 It is important for us to stay in control and manage our anger so we do not hurt others.
text: 💡 Kubalulekile kuthi ukuba sihlale sizibambile kwaye silawula imisindo ukwenzela singalimazi abanye.

SourceText: True or False: When I am angry at my teen, I should show that I am angry so that they stop misbehaving.
text: Yinyani okanye ayonyani: Xana ndinomsindo emntwaneni wam ofikisayo, Ndiyabonisa ukuba ndinomsindo ukwenzela bayeke ukuziphatha kakubi.

SourceText: 💛 It can be very difficult when your teen is misbehaving, but you should try to manage your own stress and control your own anger before doing anything.
text: 💛Kunganzima xana umntwana wakho ofikisayo eziphatha kakubi, kodwa kufuneka uzame ukulawula uxinzelelo kwaye sibambe umsindo wethu phambi kokwenza nantoni.

SourceText: {F2_GRANDMOTHER} is about to spend one-on-one time with {F2_SON_15} but finds the living room in a mess. She notices that this makes her feel angry.
text: {F2_GRANDMOTHER} imalunga nokuchitha ixesha kunye no {F2_SON_15} ninodwa kodwa ukufumana igumbi lokuhlala limdaka. Uqaphele ukuba imenza azive enomsindo.

SourceText: **{F2_GRANDMOTHER}** (thinking): “I’m feeling really angry. I am tired and my body feels tense. I just want to shout at {F2_SON_15}”
text: **{F2_GRANDMOTHER}** (ecinga): "Ndiziva ndinomsindo ngenene. Ndidiniwe kwaye umzimba wam uziva ubambekile. Ndifuna ukukhwaza u{F2_SON_15}”

SourceText: **{F2_GRANDMOTHER}** (thinking): “I will Take a Pause and put on my favourite song.”
text: **{F2_GRANDMOTHER}**(ecinga):"Ndizakuthi nqumama kwaye ndivule umculo wam endiwuthandayo."

SourceText: **{F2_GRANDMOTHER}** (thinking): “I am feeling a little better. I can think more clearly about what to do to support my teen.”
text: **{F2_GRANDMOTHER}**(ecinga):"Ndiziva ndingcono kancinci. Ndiyakwazi ukucinga ngokucacileyo malunga ndingenza ntoni ukuxhasa umntwana wam."

SourceText: **{F2_GRANDMOTHER}**: “{F2_SON_15}, well done for doing your homework. You might  feel tired now. Let's take a 5-minute break, and after that, we can quickly pick up the mess together.”
text: **{F2_GRANDMOTHER}**:  “{F2_SON_15}, wenze kakuhle ukwenza umsebenzi wakho wesikolo. Inokuba uziva udiniwe. Makhe sithathe imizuzu emi- 5 yokuphumla, emva koko, singake sichola-chole izinto kunye."

SourceText: **{F2_GRANDMOTHER}**: “Thank you for doing your part to keep the house clean, {F2_SON_15}! I’m so proud of you for helping. Oh! It is our one-on-one time. What would you like to do?”
text: **{F2_GRANDMOTHER}**: "Enkosi ngokwenza eyakho ingxenye ukugcina indlu icocekile, {F2_SON_15}! Ndiyazingca ngawe ngokuncedisa. Oh! lixesha lethu sisodwa. Ungathanda senze ntoni?"

SourceText: There are 4️⃣ steps to keeping calm in stressful situations: BE CALM, QUICK, CLEAR, and POSITIVE.
text: Kukho amanqanaba 4️⃣ okukugcina uzolile ngelixa loxinzelelo: ZOLA, KHAWULEZA, CACISA, kunye ILUNGILE.

SourceText: Home Activity! Remember to use these four steps if you start feeling stressed or angry during one-on-one time with your teen. You can also share them with another member of your family. Sharing these lessons will be great for everyone’s well-being. ➡️
text: Umsebenzi Wasekhaya! Khumbula ukusebenzisa amanqanaba amane ukuba uqala ukuziva uxinzelekile okanye unomsindo ngelixa ninodwa kunye nomntwana wakho ofikisayo. Ungakwazi nokwabelana ngazo kunye nelinye ilungu losapho. Ukwabelana ngezi zifundo kungalunga kakhulu kwimpilo yomntu wonke.➡️

SourceText: Amazing! ✨ Over the next few days, try to keep calm and redirect your teen from negative behaviour to a positive one before it gets worse.

SourceText: That's OK! Sometimes it takes time to begin using a new parenting lesson. 📝 Start by practising to Take a Pause even when you feel okay. This will help you be more ready when you feel stressed. ⭐

SourceText: ⭐ Before you focus on supporting others, it’s important to take care of yourself.
text: ⭐ Ngaphambi kokuba ugxile ekuxhaseni abanye, kubalulekile ukuba uzikhathalele.

SourceText: 😣 Feeling stressed as your children grow up is normal. As your relationship with them changes, it’s important to handle this shift well, and that starts with self-care.
text: 😣 Ukuziva unoxinzelelo njengoko abantwana bakho bekhula kuqhelekile. Njengoko nobudlelwane bakho nabo butshintsha, kubalulekile ukuphatha olutshintsho kakuhle, kwaye oko kuqala ngokuzikhathalela.

SourceText: 🌱 Self-care means doing things for yourself to reduce stress and make you feel better.
text: 🌱 Ukuzikhathalela kuthetha ukuzenzela izinto ngokwakho ukunciphisa uxinzelelo kwaye kukwenza uzive ungcono.

SourceText: ⏰ Let’s learn a simple way to handle everyday stress: Taking a pause.
text: ⏰ Masifunde indlela elula yokumelana noxinzelelo lwamihla yonke: Ukuthatha ikhefu.

SourceText: ♻ We will use this skill frequently in future lessons, so it is important to learn it today!
text: ♻ Sizakusebenzisa esisakhono rhoqo kwizifundo ezizayo, ke kubalulekile ukusifunda namhlanje!

SourceText: Taking a pause will help you manage stress and be more able to take care of others and yourself.🌱
text: Ukunqumama kuya kukunceda ulawule uxinzelelo kwaye ukwazi ngakumbi ukukhathalela abanye kunye nawe. 🌱

SourceText: ▶️Press Play to practise taking a pause together.
text: ▶️ Cofa Dlala ukuzama ukunqumama kunye...

SourceText: Try taking a pause whenever you feel angry, overwhelmed, stressed or worried. ⏰
text: Zama ukuthetha nangeliphina ixesha uziva unomsindo, udiniwe, unoxinzelelo okanye ukhathazekile.⏰

SourceText: Even a few deep breaths or connecting with the ground beneath you can make a difference. You can also take a pause with your child or teen.
text: Nokuba kukuphefumla kambalwa okanye nokunxulumana kunye nomhlaba ngaphantsi kungenza umehluko. Ungathi nqumama kunye nomntwana wakho okanye ofikisayo.

SourceText: Try taking a pause on a daily basis, even if you are feeling calm. Doing this regularly will help your brain take a pause, even in challenging situations.
text: Zama ukuthi nqumama ngemihla ngemihla, nokuba uziva uzolile. Ukwenza lento rhoqo kunganceda ingqondo yakho ithi nqumama, nakwizimo ezingumceli-mngeni.

SourceText: Well done! 🎉 You have learnt the importance of caring for yourself and how to take a pause. ✨
text: Wenza kakuhle 🎉 Ufundile ukubaleleka kokuzikhathalela wena nokuba unthi nqumama njani. ✨

SourceText: 1️⃣ **SIT DOWN**
text: 1️⃣ **HLALA PHANTSI**

SourceText: ⏸  Find a place where you can sit comfortably.
text: ⏸ Fumana indawo onokuhlala kuyo ngokukhululekileyo.

SourceText: ⭐ You can close your eyes if you want.
text: ⭐ Ungavala amehlo akho ukuba uyafuna.

SourceText: 2️⃣ **LISTEN**
text: 2️⃣ **MAMELA**

SourceText: 🌱 Take a deep breath.
text: 🌱 Thatha umphefumlo onzulu.

SourceText: 👂 Feel the air moving in and out of your body.
text: 👂 Yiva umoya ungena kwaye uphuma emzimbeni wakho.

SourceText: 🔄 Repeat this 5 times.
text: 🔄 Phinda oku ka-5.

SourceText: 3️⃣ **NOTICE**
text: 3️⃣ **QAPHELA**

SourceText: ✨ Notice how your body feels while you breathe.
text: ✨ Qaphela indlela umzimba wakho oziva ngayo ngelixa uphefumla.

SourceText: 🔵 Notice where you feel tension in your body and try to let it go.
text: 🔵 Qaphela apho uva uxinzelelo emzimbeni wakho kwaye uzame ukuwuyeka.

SourceText: 4️⃣ **OPEN YOUR EYES**
